title: To Be Carved On A Stone At Thoor Ballylee
content:
I, The poet William Yeats,
With old mill boards and sea-green slates,
And smithy work from the Gort forge,
Restored this tower for my wife George;
And may these characters remain
When all is ruin once again.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Butler Yeats, titled "To Be Carved On A Stone At Thoor Ballylee", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
